title: This is why most people quit automated trading too early
author: Feisty-Quantity9940
selftext:
I started tracking a rules-based strategy publicly because most bot content online only shows perfect entries and green days.  
Reality so far:  
historical drawdown: \~15%  
some weeks completely flat  
performance changes a lot depending on market conditions  
emotionally harder to follow than expected  
Still interesting to watch how a fully systematic approach behaves live compared to backtests.  
Curious how many people here would actually tolerate a -10% to -15% drawdown before quitting.

comments.author: QuantEdgeNewsletter
comments.body: Most people quit because they expect automated trading to produce smooth profits all the [time.In](http://time.In) reality, -10% to -15% drawdowns and flat weeks are completely normal. Usually the issue isn t the system... it s the trader who can t handle the discomfort long enough for the edge to show...

comments.author: NoBlood8896
comments.body: Automated trading has mad potential but people usually don’t understand it’s still one piece in a puzzle. You can automate and still be trash because you don’t know what your even searching for necessarily

replies.author: kwame1776
replies.body: Exactly. You’ve got to know which aspects to automate and which to handle yourself. If you automate the wrong things, you’ll blow up the account even faster.

replies.author: NoBlood8896
replies.body: Kind of but a little more nuanced than that. You technically don’t have to do anything but changes and tweaks if you understand the mechanics of what’s happening per trade you take. All that stuff sure can be done manually but as we’ve learned lol it’s 2026. Just about anything done manually can be done automatically.

replies.author: kwame1776
replies.body: Consider the simplest case: adding a SL and a TP. This is a kind of automation and doing this one thing alone, would take care of about half of the trading errors committed by beginners and many experienced traders . Think of how much more improvement you’d have by adding even more automations.

comments.author: Fun-Society-1763
comments.body: Spot on. The infra work is what really kills the motivation. People spend 90% of their time just managing data pipelines and building backtesters instead of actually trading. Tools like QuantPlace are a lifesaver here because they just give you the clean data and a backtester right out of the box.

comments.author: misscoffeeaddict2
comments.body: that 15% drawdown is exactly where most people break and switch strategies, which ironically misses the recovery. i have been using signalwhisper alongside my own rules for this exact reason. having an independent ai tool for chart analysis and market intelligence gives you a second opinion during those flat weeks so you can figure out if the market regime shifted or if it is just normal variance. it definitely helps with the emotional side of holding through a drawdown.
